title: Pteracanthus nobilis
intro: Pteracanthus nobilis, also known as Kalmegh or Andrographis paniculata, is a flowering plant species in the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Pteracanthus nobilis include andrographolide, a sesquiterpene lactone responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as andrographolide glycosides and flavonoids. Traditional herbal preparations made from Pteracanthus nobilis include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pteracanthus nobilis is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The classification of Pteracanthus nobilis is further supported by its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system.
